perturbation.original_text: d. any related exchanges of Information, clarifications, clearances, discussions, due diligence, meetings, and/or negotiations in respect of the RFP, a Project Contract, the Project Commissioning Process and/or the Project; “Receiving Party” means any Party that receives Information pursuant to this Agreement; “RFP” means the request for proposals relating to the Project, issued by the ETI on 20th April 2016 ; and “Third Party IP” means any intell ectual property rights that are not licensed or owned by a Respondent and identified by a Respondent as relevant to its Proposal.
perturbation.changed_text: d. any related exchanges of Information, clarifications, clearances, discussions, due diligence, meetings, and/or negotiations in respect of the RFP, a Project Contract, the Project Commissioning Process and/or the Project; “Receiving Party” means any Party that receives Information pursuant to this Agreement; “RFP” means the request for proposals; and “Third Party IP” means any intell ectual property rights that are not licensed or owned by a Respondent and identified by a Respondent as relevant to its Proposal.
perturbation.explanation: By generalizing the definition of “RFP” to "the request for proposals" without the specific date or context of the ETI's project, we introduce a contradiction. The contract references the “RFP” throughout, implying a specific document with defined criteria. This change makes it unclear which 'request for proposals' the agreement refers to, if any, creating an in-text contradiction where the term is used assuming a specific document, but the definition is now broad.
perturbation.contradicted_text: d. any related exchanges of Information, clarifications, clearances, discussions, due diligence, meetings, and/or negotiations in respect of the RFP, a Project Contract, the Project Commissioning Process and/or the Project; “Receiving Party” means any Party that receives Information pursuant to this Agreement; “RFP” means the request for proposals; and “Third Party IP” means any intell ectual property rights that are not licensed or owned by a Respondent and identified by a Respondent as relevant to its Proposal.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined "RFP" as a specific request for proposals issued by the ETI on a specific date (20th April 2016). The changed text broadens the definition of "RFP" to mean any request for proposals, thereby creating a contradiction. The contradicted text depends on the assumption that “RFP” refers to a particular, well-defined document, but the modification to the definition makes it unclear which 'request for proposals' the agreement refers to, creating an in-text contradiction based on differing definitions of key terms, which is covered by the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction as it omits specifics referenced elswhere.

perturbation.original_text: 2. The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication or otherwise to any third p arty (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose; b. make copies of the Information (or any fur ther information derived from the Information) in whatever form or medium only to the extent that the copies are reasonably necessary for the Purpose and clearly mark all such copies as confidential; c. take all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information) to safeguard every part of the Information to prevent it from being disclosed or otherwise made available to any third party except as permitted by this Agreement; and
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication or otherwise to any third p arty (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose; b. make copies of the Information (or any fur ther information derived from the Information) in whatever form or medium only to the extent that the copies are reasonably necessary for any activity and clearly mark all such copies as confidential; c. take all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information) to safeguard every part of the Information to prevent it from being disclosed or otherwise made available to any third party except as permitted by this Agreement; and
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Purpose' to 'any activity' in clause 2b expands the scope of permitted information use. However, clause 2a still restricts information use to 'carrying out the Purpose'. This creates a direct in-text contradiction: one clause limits use to a defined purpose, while the adjacent clause permits use for any activity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication or otherwise to any third p arty (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose; b. make copies of the Information (or any fur ther information derived from the Information) in whatever form or medium only to the extent that the copies are reasonably necessary for any activity and clearly mark all such copies as confidential; c. take all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information) to safeguard every part of the Information to prevent it from being disclosed or otherwise made available to any third party except as permitted by this Agreement; and
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies clause 2b to permit the use of information for 'any activity,' while clause 2a, which is also the 'Contradicted Text', restricts the use of information to 'carrying out the Purpose.' This creates a direct in-text contradiction because one part of the clause (2a) limits the use, and another part of the same clause (2b) expands it. The modified text introduces conflicting obligations regarding information use, as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The ETI shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Information it receives from the Respondent to: a. such of the ETI Affiliates, and either the ETI’s or the ETI Affiliates’ employees, officers, secondees, agents, consultants, su b-contractors, proposed sub -contractors, professional advisers and proposed professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary for the Purpose, provided that all such aforementioned persons to whom any Information is disclosed by the ETI are bound by obligations of confidentiality and the ETI shall be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons and each ETI Affiliate may enforce this clause in accordance with the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999; and
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The ETI shall be entitled to disclose or make available any Information it receives from the Respondent to: a. such of the ETI Affiliates, and either the ETI’s or the ETI Affiliates’ employees, officers, secondees, agents, consultants, su b-contractors, proposed sub -contractors, professional advisers and proposed professional advisers where such disclosure is necessary, provided that all such aforementioned persons to whom any Information is disclosed by the ETI are bound by obligations of confidentiality and the ETI shall be responsible for breaches of the obligations by such persons and each ETI Affiliate may enforce this clause in accordance with the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999; and
perturbation.explanation: Removing "for the Purpose" from clause 5a broadens the ETI's ability to disclose information without a defined context or activity. This creates a contradiction with the opening statement of clause 2 which dictates the Information shall be used to only carry out the 'Purpose'. This directly contradicts the later clause 5a and introduces ambiguity and in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication or otherwise to any third p arty (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose; b. make copies of the Information (or any fur ther information derived from the Information) in whatever form or medium only to the extent that the copies are reasonably necessary for any activity and clearly mark all such copies as confidential; c. take all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information) to safeguard every part of the Information to prevent it from being disclosed or otherwise made available to any third party except as permitted by this Agreement; and
perturbation.justification: The removal of "for the Purpose" from clause 5a broadens the ETI's ability to disclose information. This conflicts with clause 2, which states that information disclosed shall only be used for carrying out the 'Purpose'. Therefore, the changed text creates a direct in-text contradiction by allowing disclosure without the 'Purpose' constraint that is required in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 10. The Parties agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Agreeme nt, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. The Parties agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedy shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity," the modified clause establishes specific performance and injunctive relief as the *only* remedies for breach of the agreement. However, this directly conflicts with clause 16, which states: 'The rights of the Disclosing Party under this Agreement are in addition to and not exclusive of rights under the general law'. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the availability of other legal remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. The rights of the Disclosing Party under this Agreement are in addition to and not exclusive of rights under the general law and may be waived only in writing and specifically. Delay in exercising or non - exercise of any right under this Agreement is not a waiver of that or any other right, partial exercise of any right under this Agreement shall not preclude any fur ther or other exercise of that right or any other right under this Agreement and waiver of a breach of any term of this Agreement shall not operate as a waiver of breach of any other term or any subsequent breach of that term.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Disclosing Party has access to specific performance/injunctive relief *in addition to* other legal remedies. The changed text removes this phrase, specifying that specific performance/injunctive relief are the *exclusive* remedies. This directly contradicts clause 16, which states that the Disclosing Party's rights under the agreement are 'in addition to and not exclusive of rights under the general law'. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the availability of other legal remedies, aligning with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 12. This Agreement represents the entire agreement between the Parties in relation to the subject matter contai ned herein and supersedes all other agreements and representations, whether oral or written, between the Parties relating to such subject matter. This Agreement may only be modified if such modification is in writing and signed by a duly authorised represe ntative of each Party. Each Party also agrees that it shall have no remedies or claims under this Agreement for any innocent or negligent misrepresentation based on statements made prior to the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. This Agreement represents the entire agreement between the Parties in relation to the subject matter contai ned herein and supersedes all other agreements and representations, whether oral or written, between the Parties relating to such subject matter. This Agreement may only be modified if such modification is agreed upon by the involved parties. Each Party also agrees that it shall have no remedies or claims under this Agreement for any innocent or negligent misrepresentation based on statements made prior to the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: The original states "This Agreement may only be modified if such modification is in writing and signed by a duly authorised representative of each Party". The modification now says "This Agreement may only be modified if such modification is agreed upon by the involved parties." There's an in-text contradiction, as it doesn't say it has to be in writing or signed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. This Agreement represents the entire agreement between the Parties in relation to the subject matter contai ned herein and supersedes all other agreements and representations, whether oral or written, between the Parties relating to such subject matter. This Agreement may only be modified if such modification is agreed upon by the involved parties. Each Party also agrees that it shall have no remedies or claims under this Agreement for any innocent or negligent misrepresentation based on statements made prior to the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires modifications to be 'in writing and signed by a duly authorised representative of each Party.' The changed text omits this requirement, stating modifications only need to be 'agreed upon by the involved parties.' This creates a contradiction because the changed text allows for oral or unsigned modifications, conflicting with the original text's explicit requirement for written and signed modifications as well as the contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text).
